rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $1,182.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim; Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Raymond Fong  And Chloe Mosqueda In Support Thereof [Proposed Order]
rulings.Rulings:
Matter on calendar for Friday, September 16, 2016, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim. 

Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: 

The motion is GRANTED.  No opposition filed.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.  =(302/JPT/HK)

Matter on calendar for Friday, September 16, 2016, Line 6, PLAINTIFF L.E.K. CONSULTING LLC Motion To Compel Responses And Production Of Documents From Judgement Debtor Menlo Capital Group, LLC (Now Known As M2 Management, LLC) And For Monetary Sanctions.

Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: 

The motion is GRANTED.  No opposition filed.  All objections are waived.  Defendant to serve verified responses to the plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents within 15 days of Notice of Entry of Order.  The plaintiff's request for sanctions in the amount of $2,500 is GRANTED.  Payment to be made within 15 days of Notice of Entry of Order.

rulings.Rulings:

The motion for bifurcation of trial is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.  The trial judge will be best-suited to decide such motions. 

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA WILLIAMS et al VS. WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Requesting An Order Ursuant  To Civil Code Section 3295(C)
rulings.Rulings:
Set for hearing on Friday, September 16, 2016,  Line 3,  PLAINTIFF PATRICIA WILLIAMS' Motion Requesting An Order Pursuant To Civil Code Section 3295(C).

The motion for a Civil Code §3295(c) order is DENIED.  After weighing the evidence presented, I cannot say that "plaintiff is very likely to prevail" on her punitive damages claim.  (See Guardado v. Sup. Ct. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 91, 98.)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA WILLIAMS et al VS. WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings:
Set for hearing on Friday, September 16, 2016,  Line 3,  DEFENDANTS WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP AND WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The motion for summary adjudication is DENIED as to the second cause of action for violation of Labor Code section 1102.5.  There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant discharged plaintiff because she complained to government entities.  At paragraphs 2-5 of her declaration, plaintiff declares that she contacted government agencies prior to her discharge and informed them of defendant's alleged fraud.  Plaintiff admits that she did not file a formal claim at that time, but the germane statute does not require a formal complaint.  P merely needed to "disclose[] information."  The court overrules defendant's objection based on D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 20.  There is no direct conflict between plaintiff's declaration and her discovery responses.  

The motion for summary adjudication as to the sixth cause of action for negligent hiring, retention and/or supervision is GRANTED.  Defendant correctly cites Hine v. Ditrich (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 59, 65, and plaintiff fails to distinguish that controlling decision.  The Second Amended Complaint alleges that defendant's negligence led to the discharge.  Under Hine, plaintiff's remedy is to pursue a claim for breach of contract, not a tort remedy for negligence.  Plaintiff can also pursue her claim for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy or the retaliation claim under the Labor Code.    

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RU)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA WILLIAMS et al VS. WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Evidence In Support Of Their Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings:
Set for hearing on Friday, September 16, 2016,  Line 3,  DEFENDANTS WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP AND WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION'S Motion To File Under Seal Evidence In Support Of Their Motion For Summary Adjudication.

The sealing motion is DENIED.  Defendant fails to meet its burden under CRC 2.550.  Defendant fails to establish an overriding interest why the ethics hotline complaint needs to be sealed.  Defendant does not clearly explain what proprietary interest is at stake.  Plaintiff's co-employees have no privacy interest in plaintiff's description of their conduct that occurred in public.  There is no chilling effect/public policy issue in this case because the person complaining on the hotline (plaintiff) does not oppose disclosure of the complaint.   

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWAN VS. DANIEL LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings:
Set for hearing on Friday, September 16, 2016,  Line 4,  DEFENDANT DANIEL LOPEZ'S Motion For Reconsideration.

Defendant Lopez concedes that (1) a party moving for reconsideration must present new facts that were unavailable to him when the Court considered the original motion and (2) the facts he now asserts were available well before the Court considered the original motion.  Further, Lopez has provided no declaration from any federal official that supports his belated factual arguments.  The motion for reconsideration is DENIED.

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN SODERSTROM VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings:
Set for hearing on Friday, September 16, 2016,  Line 6,  PLAINTIFF SUSAN SODERSTROM'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES.

The motion is GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY.  Plaintiff's counsel achieved an good result for her: more money than she paid for her car, after years of using it.  Defendant challenges no specific time entry by plaintiff's counsel.  Counsel's rates are reasonable for the San Francisco legal market.  Defendant challenges none of plaintiff's costs or expenses.

Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: 

The motion is continued to September 27, 2016, because the Judge Pro Tem recuses himself.  

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.  =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MARCELO COSTAS VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval Of The Parties Labor Code Private Attorneys General Act Settlement - Also Filed By Defendants
rulings.Rulings:
Set for hearing on Friday, September 16, 2016,  Line 7,  PLAINTIFF MARCELO COSTAS' Joint  Motion For Court Approval Of The Parties Labor Code Private Attorneys General Act Settlement.

The motion, to which no opposition was filed, is GRANTED.  

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.  =(302/RU)
